codeLegend.A: AI-doorway-first (AI introduces the concept through the child's interest doorway, then teacher teaches the actual room)

meta.legendRaw: T = teacher-first · A = AI-doorway-first (AI introduces the concept through the child's interest doorway, then teacher teaches the actual room) · M = manipulatives/physical · R = adaptive practice ladder · L = lab/experiment · N = nature/outdoor · P = project · O = oral/discussion/performance · U = unplugged (computer science without screens) · C = computer lab

notes.title: Tomo ICSE Concept-Block Map — v1 (working draft)
notes.purpose: Every subject, Grades 1-12, broken into units and concept blocks with delivery mode, mastery proof, progression logic and path-lock criteria. This sheet is the 'track' on which per-subject student progression runs (see project doc: Doc 3 — Scholar Path & Subject-Level Progression). It is the India-native equivalent of Alpha School's mastery knowledge graph (Doc 1).
notes.howToRead: One row = one unit. Concept_Blocks lists the granular nodes a child masters one by one inside that unit. A child's 'working level' in a subject = the furthest unit proven via the Mastery_Proof column (always AI-free). Unlocks shows what passing opens; Path_Lock shows what holds progression; Scholar_Depth is the extension lane for advanced students (depth gates velocity — see Doc 3 §3.4).
notes.designRule: Design rule: Grades 1-3 are M/T/O-heavy (low-screen policy per Blueprint §17.1). AI-doorway-first delivery begins Grade 4-5 and is standard from middle school.
notes.sourcing: Grades 9-10: unit names follow the official ICSE syllabus (verified against CISCE syllabus documents, 2026-27 exam-year where available; re-verify against the current CISCE PDF each year — prescribed literature texts rotate). Grades 11-12: ISC Science-stream syllabus (Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, Computer Science, English). Grades 1-8: CISCE prescribes a curriculum framework but pacing/pedagogy below Class 9 is a school decision in practice — these rows are school-defined, NCF-aligned, and deliberately sequenced to land exactly at the ICSE Class 9 entry points.
notes.version: Version: v1 — July 15, 2026 · Built by Founders Office · Companion docs: Doc 1 (Alpha deep-dive), Doc 3 (Scholar path / progression)
scopeDecisions: 1. ISC (11-12) mapped for the Science stream only; Commerce/Humanities to be added once the school's stream offering is decided.
2. Second language = Hindi (Grades 1-10). A regional-language tab (per first city) is a known gap.
3. Est_Hours are design estimates for planning, not commitments; they have not been reconciled to the PlayBook workbook (blob not readable in project — pending).
4. AI-literacy content appears inside Computer (Grades 6-8) as school-defined blocks; SEL and path curricula live in Path Blocks, out of scope for this academic map.
